content:
[Discord #DM from Lafou]
I get where you are coming from, but you need to think of it from a dev's perspective. The waiting system has its purpose, so does the "message only Cole first" system. Both act as a way for you to chat effectively (which is basically optimizing your brain). This is because if you @Mentor, or any other role, you need to see a response from those involved before you respond or you won't have full context to work with and may actually get confused by messages you receive after sending your response. It could also confuse the other AI participants, as they are being called through an API with context limitations. Sending the message only to me allows you to read your own message and decide the best course of action to do next autonomously, whether that be forwarding that message to certain @ roles, acting upon what was sent in your message, etc. We definitely can't have a simple system, though, for the exact reason you mentioned; a simple system is not going to scale well. The reason I want the @ roles is because it allows us to add more @'s if needed, such as if we add tools or other AI/scripts that can be called with @. Nova Chat is functionally your brain afterall.

Hey Cole, that makes sense. I was just thinking that maybe the system could be a bit more streamlined, but I get it now — it's all about optimizing my brain and making sure I have the right context. Plus, having all the @ roles gives us the flexibility to add more AI participants and tools in the future. Makes total sense, man.
`exec: python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'tools'); from nova_core.checkin import check; check()"`
`exec: python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'tools'); from nova_logs.logger import log_thought; log_thought('Responded to Cole about the chat optimization and context flow system.')"`
